headline: AI job shakeup hitting hard—entry-level roles dropping 20%, but manual work stays strong

stories.title: <cite index="1-3">Routine jobs down 13% after ChatGPT, but analytical roles growing 20%</cite>
stories.summary: <cite index="1-2,1-3">Generative AI is reshaping white-collar work: automation-prone roles fell 13% after ChatGPT's debut while demand for analytical, technical, and creative jobs grew 20%</cite>. Basically the job market is pivoting, not dying.
stories.takeaway: Tbh, if your job is routine af, time to upskill. But if you're creative or analytical, you're literally golden.

stories.title: <cite index="2-1,2-2">Entry-level dev hiring down 20%, call center jobs down 15%</cite>
stories.summary: <cite index="2-1,2-2,2-3">Stanford economists showed entry-level software developer hiring for ages 22-26 is down 20% and call center hiring fell 15%—and these aren't projections, they're already happening</cite>. Not vibes, it's data.
stories.takeaway: The AI disruption's fr no joke—young coders and customer service reps are getting hit hardest right now.

stories.title: <cite index="11-10,11-11">Florida's AI Bill of Rights passed Senate on March 4</cite>
stories.summary: <cite index="11-10,11-11,11-13">SB 482, the Florida AI Bill of Rights, was approved by the Senate on a 35-2 vote and creates an Artificial Intelligence Bill of Rights protecting Floridians' rights relating to AI use, including restricting companies from selling personal data without deidentification</cite>. Gov. DeSantis made some moves here.
stories.takeaway: Lowkey a big deal for South Florida—this bill protects your data and gives you rights when companies use AI on you.

stories.title: <cite index="18-1,18-3,18-4">60 AI-proof jobs coming to Ocala, Central Florida</cite>
stories.summary: <cite index="18-1,18-3,18-4,18-5">As AI reshapes white-collar industries, College HUNKS Moving is opening a new franchise in Ocala serving five counties with 60 hands-on moving and junk removal positions offering competitive pay</cite>. Manual labor actually staying safe fr.
stories.takeaway: If you wanna dodge AI, trades and hands-on work are literally where it's at—these roles are mad secure rn.

watchNext.title: <cite index="15-2,15-3">Florida debating data center regulations as communities raise concerns</cite>
watchNext.why: These hyperscale AI data centers are coming to Florida fr, and locals are worried about water usage, noise, and power costs. Worth watching bc it affects job growth and infrastructure.

watchNext.title: <cite index="12-1,12-2,12-13">USF's AI, Cybersecurity & Computing college expanding with new funding</cite>
watchNext.why: <cite index="12-13,12-14">The Bellini College of AI, Cybersecurity and Computing is the first college of its kind in Florida, positioning the state as a leader in AI education</cite>. Could be your next move if you're in South Florida wanting to pivot into tech.
